title: Java Scoped Values
description: Share immutable context for a dynamic scope with ScopedValue.
learningGoals: Bind an immutable value for the duration of a call with ScopedValue (JEP 506, final in Java 25).
Read the bound value downstream with get() instead of threading it through parameters.

completionChecks.prompt: What does currentUser() print inside the ScopedValue.where(...).run(...) call?
choices: It throws because USER is never bound
completionChecks.explanation: ScopedValue.where(...).run(...) binds the value for the dynamic extent of that call, then the binding ends.

completionChecks.prompt: What kind of sharing are scoped values designed for?
choices: Immutable context passed down a call tree
Mutable counters shared by every thread
completionChecks.explanation: Scoped values provide immutable, bounded context that downstream code can read without mutable shared state.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if currentUser() is called after ScopedValue.where(...).run(...) has returned?
choices: NoSuchElementException is thrown because USER is no longer bound
It prints ada because the binding is stored globally
It returns null because the scoped value was cleared
It fails to compile because get() is only allowed inside lambdas
completionChecks.explanation: The binding lasts only for the dynamic extent of run, so the trap is assuming a thread-local-style leftover value.
